statement: AMD's China export controls restrict Instinct MI308 and require licenses for MI325; AMD incurred approximately $440 million of net inventory and related charges in 2025, and any MI325 shipped to China must first undergo US inspection and faces a 25% tariff.
why: Export licensing requirements and tariffs reduce the China datacenter-GPU revenue AMD can serve and impose direct inventory and charge costs.

impact.mechanism: License requirements, US-inspection tariffs and Entity List controls shrink AMD's addressable China accelerator demand and add cost.

evidence.excerpt: we incurred approximately $440 million of net inventory and related charges in 2025
